questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: According to the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Rules 2025, what is the implementation timeline provided for organizations to adjust their systems?
questions.explanation: The DPDP Rules 2025 specify an 18-month phased compliance period. This allows organizations time to adjust their technical systems and adopt the responsible data practices required by the Act.
questions.takeaway: The 2025 Rules provide a specific 18-month window for phased implementation.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Implementation timeline: 18-month phased compliance period for organizations to adjust their systems and adopt responsible data practices.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: Under the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023, in which of the following scenarios is the 'Consent' of the Data Principal NOT mandatory for processing personal data?

options.text: Processing for the purpose of targeted commercial advertising by a private entity.
Processing for the provision of any benefit or service by the Government.
Processing for academic research by a non-governmental organization.
Processing for the creation of a new customer profile by a financial institution.

questions.explanation: The Act specifies certain 'legitimate uses' where consent is not required. These include government benefits, medical emergencies, and employment-related purposes. Commercial advertising, academic research by NGOs, and general customer profiling do not fall under these specific exemptions.
questions.takeaway: Consent is the default requirement, but 'legitimate uses' like government services and emergencies are statutory exceptions.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Data processed only for lawful purpose with consent, withdrawable anytime. Consent not required for legitimate uses (government benefits, medical emergencies, etc.).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: In the event of a personal data breach, what is the primary obligation of a 'Data Fiduciary' under the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act, 2023?

options.text: To inform only the Data Protection Board of India within 72 hours.
To inform the Data Protection Board of India and each affected Data Principal.
To immediately erase all data related to the breach to prevent further exposure.
To compensate the affected Data Principals before notifying the regulatory authorities.

questions.explanation: According to the Act, in the case of a data breach, the Data Fiduciary is obligated to inform both the Data Protection Board of India (DPBI) and the affected individuals (Data Principals).
questions.takeaway: Transparency in data breaches is a dual obligation involving notification to both the regulator and the victim.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Obligations of Data Fiduciaries: Inform DPBI and affected persons in case of breach.
